nodes.name: medical_imaging_3d_augmentation
nodes.description: Shape-preserving 3D augmentation operations for medical and tomographic volumes.
nodes.conceptual_summary: Stateless augmentation atoms from the 3D volumetric augmentation research packet.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: Each atom accepts an in-memory array and explicit parameters, including seeds for random operations, and returns one transformed array.

nodes.name: random_rotate_3d
nodes.description: Rotates a 3D volume around the geometric center with one inverse affine interpolation pass.

inputs.name: volume
inputs.constraints: real numeric array with at least three non-empty spatial axes
inputs.provenance: pre-loaded cryo-ET, CT, MRI, or tomographic patch

inputs.name: angles_deg
inputs.constraints: three finite Euler angles in degrees

nodes.type_signature: (volume: NDArray[np.number], angles_deg: tuple[float, float, float], order: int = 1) -> NDArray[np.float64]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Builds a 3D Euler rotation matrix and samples around the volume center.
nodes.critic_notes: For Vesuvius-style surface volumes, callers should restrict angles to Z-axis quarter-turns or use a specialized policy layer.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: The atom isolates only the numeric resampling operation and does not choose augmentation probabilities.

nodes.name: elastic_deform_3d
nodes.description: Warps a volume with a seeded, Gaussian-smoothed displacement field.

inputs.name: volume
inputs.constraints: real numeric array with at least three non-empty spatial axes
inputs.provenance: pre-loaded cryo-ET, CT, MRI, or tomographic patch

inputs.name: seed
inputs.constraints: integer seed for local random generator

nodes.type_signature: (volume: NDArray[np.number], sigma: float, alpha: float, seed: int, order: int = 1) -> NDArray[np.float64]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Samples a local random displacement tensor, smooths it, and maps coordinates through SciPy interpolation.
nodes.critic_notes: Large alpha values are unsuitable for rigid molecular structures such as cryo-ET flagellar motors.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: The atom implements deterministic field construction and sampling while leaving modality-specific parameter ranges to callers.

nodes.name: scale_volume_3d
nodes.description: Zooms the last three axes and restores the original bounds by center crop or zero padding.

inputs.name: volume
inputs.constraints: real numeric array with at least three non-empty spatial axes
inputs.provenance: pre-loaded cryo-ET, CT, MRI, or tomographic patch

outputs.name: result
outputs.provenance: scipy zoom followed by deterministic recentering

nodes.type_signature: (volume: NDArray[np.number], scale_factors: tuple[float, float, float], order: int = 1) -> NDArray[np.float64]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Uses SciPy zoom, then crops or pads symmetrically to maintain batchable tensor dimensions.
nodes.critic_notes: No anti-aliasing prefilter is applied before severe downscaling.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: The atom separates pure dimensional augmentation from downstream model batching and spacing metadata.

nodes.name: add_gaussian_noise_3d
nodes.description: Adds seeded Gaussian intensity noise without touching global random state.

inputs.name: volume
inputs.constraints: real numeric array with at least three non-empty spatial axes
inputs.provenance: pre-loaded cryo-ET, CT, MRI, or tomographic patch

inputs.name: seed
inputs.constraints: integer seed for local random generator

outputs.name: result
outputs.constraints: same shape and dtype as volume
outputs.provenance: local NumPy generator normal sample added to volume

nodes.type_signature: (volume: NDArray[np.number], mean: float, std: float, seed: int) -> NDArray[np.number]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Creates an isolated NumPy generator and adds one Gaussian noise tensor.
nodes.critic_notes: Callers must choose std from modality-specific normalization or SNR calibration.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: The atom isolates deterministic intensity perturbation and avoids global RNG coupling.
